task_completion.description: Open the Landsat multispectral image in ESA SNAP Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: ESA SNAP Desktop is open and 'landsat_multispectral' is visible in the Product Explorer panel.

task_completion.description: Compute the NDVI band using the specified formula.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Band Math' dialog is visible showing the target band name 'ndvi' and the expression '(band_2 - band_3) / (band_2 + band_3)', or an 'ndvi' band is visible under the Bands folder in the Product Explorer.

task_completion.description: Create a mask to isolate dense vegetation where ndvi > 0.4.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Mask Manager panel or a logical band math dialog is visible showing a mask created with the condition 'ndvi > 0.4'.

task_completion.description: Convert the dense vegetation mask into vector geometries.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A tool like 'Geometry from Mask' is being used, or a new vector data node (representing the mask) is visible in the Vector Data folder of the Product Explorer.

task_completion.description: Export the vector data as an ESRI Shapefile to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog for ESRI Shapefile is visible with the path ending in 'snap_exports/dense_vegetation.shp', or a file manager/terminal shows the file has been created.

task_completion.description: Save the project in BEAM-DIMAP format to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Product' or 'Save Project' dialog is visible with the path ending in 'snap_projects/vegetation_analysis.dim', or the product name is updated in the Product Explorer.

integrity.description: The agent used the ESA SNAP Desktop GUI to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with SNAP Desktop menus, dialogs (Band Math, Mask Manager), and panels, rather than using terminal commands (e.g., gdal, snappy) to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent actually computed the band and mask rather than creating dummy files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The SNAP interface should show the actual image data, computed NDVI band, and vector geometries being rendered or processed in the main view or dialogs.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a text editor to write a Python or shell script to solve the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like nano, vim, gedit) are shown writing scripts to generate the .dim or .shp files directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input file is located at /home/ga/snap_data/landsat_multispectral.tif. The exact NDVI expression required is '(band_2 - band_3) / (band_2 + band_3)'. The mask condition must be 'ndvi > 0.4'. The final vector export must be saved to /home/ga/snap_exports/dense_vegetation.shp and the SNAP project must be saved to /home/ga/snap_projects/vegetation_analysis.dim.
